data.title: Jupiter Neurosciences Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Jupiter Neurosciences, Inc. ( (JUNS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jupiter Neurosciences, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company’s annual filing for this period, originally due March 31, 2026, is therefore being submitted after the standard deadline.

The primary reason for the delay is administrative rather than financial or operational. Management states that the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) was substantially completed, but the company missed the SEC’s 5:30 p.m. Eastern Time electronic filing cutoff and thus could not submit on time without unreasonable effort or expense.

Jupiter Neurosciences indicates that it is finalizing the documentation and expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) by April 1, 2026. Under the 12b-25 relief, the company has up to 15 calendar days after the prescribed due date for an annual report, and it has confirmed its intent to remain within this extended window.

The company does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, based on the information to be included in the upcoming Form 10-K (Yearly Report). However, investors should note that any expectations about timing and financial outcomes remain forward-looking and could be affected by subsequent events or final audit conclusions.

Jupiter Neurosciences affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling an otherwise consistent compliance history. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Christer Rosén on April 1, 2026, underscoring management’s commitment to completing the annual reporting process and maintaining regulatory compliance.

The most recent analyst rating on (JUNS) stock is a Buy with a $25.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jupiter Neurosciences, Inc. stock, see the JUNS Stock Forecast page.

More about Jupiter Neurosciences, Inc.

Jupiter Neurosciences, Inc. is a U.S.-based biotechnology company focused on developing neuroscience-related therapies. It is listed as a registrant under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and files annual and periodic reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The company operates from its principal executive office in Jupiter, Florida.

Average Trading Volume: 320,831

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $12.95M

For an in-depth examination of JUNS stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Charlotte’s Web Holdings ( (TSE:CWEB) ) has provided an update.

Charlotte’s Web Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-based producer of hemp-derived wellness products, focused on cannabidiol (CBD) formulations for food, dietary supplements and related consumer health applications. The company targets regulated cannabis-adjacent markets and lists its common shares on the Toronto Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, positioning itself as a branded CBD platform for mainstream retail channels.

On March 30, 2026, Charlotte’s Web agreed to a C$13.9 million equity investment from British American Tobacco subsidiary BT DE Investments, alongside an amendment to a 2022 C$75.3 million convertible debenture that cuts the conversion price to C$0.94 and lifts BAT’s potential stake to about 40.8% on a non‑diluted basis. Upon an expected closing around May 28, 2026, BAT will immediately convert the debenture into roughly 95.3 million shares, gaining board nomination, pre‑emptive and minority‑protection rights under an amended investor rights agreement, while accepting standstill, lock‑up and ownership limits that cement it as a controlling strategic partner without crossing a 49% threshold.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CWEB) stock is a Hold with a C$0.75 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Charlotte’s Web Holdings stock, see the TSE:CWEB Stock Forecast page.

The company targets regulated cannabis-adjacent markets and lists its common shares on the Toronto Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, positioning itself as a branded CBD platform for mainstream retail channels.

On March 30, 2026, Charlotte’s Web agreed to a C$13.9 million equity investment from British American Tobacco subsidiary BT DE Investments, alongside an amendment to a 2022 C$75.3 million convertible debenture that cuts the conversion price to C$0.94 and lifts BAT’s potential stake to about 40.8% on a non‑diluted basis. Upon an expected closing around May 28, 2026, BAT will immediately convert the debenture into roughly 95.3 million shares, gaining board nomination, pre‑emptive and minority‑protection rights under an amended investor rights agreement, while accepting standstill, lock‑up and ownership limits that cement it as a controlling strategic partner without crossing a 49% threshold.

data.title: Black Hawk Acquisition Faces Nasdaq Listing Compliance Challenge
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation Class A ( (BKHA) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation disclosed that it had received a notice from Nasdaq indicating the company had failed to meet the $50 million market value of listed securities requirement for the prior 30 consecutive business days, putting its Nasdaq Global Market listing at risk. The company has until September 28, 2026, to regain compliance through a sustained recovery in market value, after which its securities could face delisting, appeal proceedings, or a potential transfer to the Nasdaq Capital Market, although trading in its shares, units, and rights continues uninterrupted in the interim.

More about Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation Class A

Black Hawk Acquisition Corporation is a special purpose acquisition company whose securities, including its Class A ordinary shares, units, and rights, are listed on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbols BKHA, BKHAU, and BKHAR. As a blank-check company, its primary purpose is to pursue business combinations, with its market value and listing status being key concerns for investors and potential targets.

data.title: Arcosa Sells Inland Barge Unit to Refocus Portfolio
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arcosa ( (ACA) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 1, 2026, Arcosa, Inc. announced it had completed the sale of its inland barge business, Arcosa Marine Products, Inc., to private equity firm Wynnchurch Capital, L.P. for $450 million in cash, subject to customary adjustments. The company said it intends to use the net after-tax proceeds to fund expansion in its core growth platforms and to reduce outstanding debt, advancing a strategy to simplify its portfolio and streamline its focus.

Chief executive Antonio Carrillo said the divestiture marks a significant milestone that lowers complexity and cyclicality, lifts margins and strengthens the company’s long-term resiliency as it concentrates on construction materials and engineered structures. In March 2026, Arcosa also completed the $60 million acquisition of a central Florida natural aggregates operation, which is expected to be margin accretive and bolsters its position in the Florida market.

Following the barge sale, Arcosa plans to update its full-year 2026 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA guidance with first-quarter results, having previously included $410 million to $430 million in revenue and $70 million to $75 million in Adjusted EBITDA from the divested inland barge unit. Results from the barge business will be reported as discontinued operations, and segment reporting for Transportation Products will be eliminated, signaling a tighter operational focus for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (ACA) stock is a Buy with a $115.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arcosa stock, see the ACA Stock Forecast page.

More about Arcosa

Arcosa focuses on serving infrastructure and power markets, particularly in the U.S., where its construction materials and engineered structures businesses are positioned to benefit from industry tailwinds. The company emphasizes high-growth, high-margin platforms as it reshapes its portfolio around these core operations.

An update from Bunge Global ( (BG) ) is now available.

On March 26, 2026, Bunge Global SA’s board approved a special, one-time Executive Integration Incentive Program granting performance-based restricted stock units to senior officers, including CEO Gregory Heckman and other named executives. The awards, tied to cumulative run-rate cost synergy targets over a performance period from January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2028, are designed to accelerate the integration of Viterra Limited and ensure sustained executive focus on achieving multi-year synergy and cost savings goals.

The PBRSUs will only vest and be settled in registered shares if the specified synergy targets are met and the executives remain employed through the vesting date, with additional constraints for CEO Heckman and CFO John Neppl linked to an existing shareholder-approved cap on total executive management compensation. This structure aligns senior leadership’s long-term incentives with the success of the Viterra integration, signaling the company’s emphasis on disciplined execution of cost synergies and tighter linkage between pay and performance outcomes for key stakeholders.

data.title: Windtree Therapeutics Delays Filing of Annual Report
data.description:
Windtree Therapeutics ( (WINT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Windtree Therapeutics, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 indicating it will be late in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The delayed filing concerns its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not a Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) or Form 20-F (Yearly Report).

The company cites limited liquidity and constrained internal resources as the main reasons for the delay. Management has been heavily focused on exploring financing options and potential strategic transactions, diverting time and staff away from the completion of year-end financial statements and disclosures.

Windtree notes that these capital-raising and strategic review efforts, while necessary for its survival, have made it impractical to finalize the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) by the standard SEC deadline without unreasonable effort or expense. There is no assurance that any strategic transaction will ultimately be completed.

The company expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before April 15, 2026, using the 15-calendar-day extension allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report becoming available within that extended window, barring unforeseen complications.

data.description:

Trex Company ( (TREX) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Trex Company, Inc. amended and restated its existing credit facility, entering a new credit agreement that increases its revolving loan capacity to $700 million from $550 million and extends the maturity to March 26, 2031. The expanded facility, which includes sublimits for a $60 million letter of credit line and $40 million in swing line loans, is secured by a broad collateral package and is designed to support refinancing of existing debt, working capital, capital expenditures, acquisitions and other corporate purposes, while imposing leverage and interest coverage covenants that will shape Trex’s balance sheet flexibility and acquisition strategy in the coming years.

Under the new agreement, Trex can borrow, repay and reborrow within the committed limit on variable terms tied to base rate and SOFR benchmarks, with pricing tiers adjusted to its consolidated debt-to-EBITDA ratio. The facility also features an equity cure mechanism and temporary leverage headroom following qualifying acquisitions, signaling lender support for Trex’s potential growth-through-acquisition plans while maintaining discipline around leverage and coverage metrics that are important for creditors and investors.

data.title: Ondas Completes World View Acquisition, Expanding Stratollite ISR
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade ONDS with leverage

Ondas ( (ONDS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 1, 2026, Ondas Inc. completed its acquisition of World View Enterprises, merging it into a wholly owned subsidiary in a transaction involving up to 12,775,219 Ondas common shares, escrowed stock for price adjustments, and approximately $7.3 million in cash toward outstanding obligations. The company simultaneously put in place registration rights and six‑month trading volume limits for certain World View shareholders and granted inducement equity awards, including RSUs and stock options, to 26 new employees tied to the deal.

The acquisition extends Ondas’ architecture into the stratosphere through World View’s Stratollite high‑altitude ISR platform, creating a persistent, AI‑enabled, multi‑domain intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance system spanning stratosphere, air, and ground. Integrated with Palantir’s software and Ondas’ existing autonomous drones, counter‑UAS systems, and robotics, the combined platform is intended to strengthen Ondas’ position in next‑generation defense and homeland security programs, expand its addressable market in layered ISR and mission‑critical decision platforms, and accelerate deployment of unified, multi‑domain solutions for global defense, government, and commercial customers.

More about Ondas

Ondas Inc., listed on Nasdaq as ONDS, operates in the defense, security, and industrial technology sectors, providing autonomous systems, robotics, and mission-critical wireless connectivity. Through units including Ondas Autonomous Systems, Ondas Capital, and Ondas Networks, it delivers AI-enabled air and ground robotic platforms, counter‑UAS technologies, advanced sensing systems, and IEEE 802.16t‑based industrial IoT communications solutions.

Its technologies support defense forces, government agencies, and industrial operators worldwide with integrated capabilities for persistent intelligence, surveillance, security, and operational response. By combining autonomous systems, advanced sensing, and resilient connectivity, Ondas targets complex operational environments and large-scale defense and critical infrastructure markets, positioning itself within the growing ecosystem of next-generation robotics and networked ISR platforms.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

JW Mays ( (MAYS) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, J.W.M. Realty Corp., a wholly owned subsidiary of J.W. Mays, Inc., entered into a $6.2 million loan agreement with Putnam County National Bank of Carmel, secured by a first mortgage on its Circleville, Ohio property and guaranteed unconditionally by J.W. Mays. The loan carries a fixed 7.00% interest rate, requires monthly payments of $48,068.53 starting May 1, 2026, matures April 1, 2031, and includes declining prepayment penalties over the first three years while allowing the lender to call the loan on demand after the term date.

The company used $3,135,704 of the proceeds on March 27, 2026 to retire an existing secured loan with the same lender and plans to allocate the remaining funds toward maintenance, repairs, and onboarding new tenants across its properties, potentially enhancing property quality and occupancy. A director of J.W. Mays, Dean L. Ryder, is affiliated with the lending bank, though the company stated the loan was made on terms generally available to other borrowers, which may help address governance and related-party concerns for investors and other stakeholders.

More about JW Mays

J.W. Mays, Inc., through its wholly owned subsidiary J.W.M. Realty Corp., operates as a property owner and manager with a portfolio that includes the Circleville, Ohio property used as collateral in this financing. The company focuses on leasing and maintaining its real estate assets and onboarding new tenants across various properties to support rental-driven revenue streams.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Micron ( (MU) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 31, 2026, Micron Technology announced the pricing of cash tender offers for any and all of six tranches of outstanding senior notes maturing between 2031 and 2035, with per-$1,000 consideration ranging from about $1,048 to nearly $1,080 based on fixed spreads over specified U.S. Treasury reference yields. The offers, launched March 25 and expiring at 5 p.m. New York time on March 31, allowed noteholders to tender or use guaranteed delivery procedures ahead of an expected April 3, 2026 settlement, in a move that fine-tunes Micron’s long-term debt profile and gives bond investors an opportunity to exit at a premium.

On April 1, 2026, Micron disclosed that these cash tender offers had expired as scheduled, with no minimum participation condition imposed on any series of notes. The transaction, which the company could amend or terminate subject to legal and customary conditions, forms part of its ongoing capital structure management and may modestly reduce interest costs and extend financial flexibility without obligating holders to participate.
